description: Current-state specification for the fswm package. Read this file as a description of the behavior, constraints, and verification surface of the code that exists today, not as a roadmap or aspirational design document.

interpretation.sourceOfTruth: This file summarizes the present implementation in main.c and the package definition in default.nix. If code and this file diverge, update the code and checks together or rewrite this file to match the current implementation.
notes: This package is a small X11 window manager written in C against XCB and xcb-keysyms.
The scenarios below describe implemented runtime behavior and current package-level verification.
Behavior is intentionally minimal; absence of a scenario should not be interpreted as an unimplemented product requirement.

requiredChecks: the package builds successfully with `nix-build default.nix --no-out-link`
compilation as C89 succeeds with warnings treated as errors
linking against XCB and xcb-keysyms succeeds
executing the built binary with no terminal command prints usage text and exits successfully
the build uses sanitizers, stack protection, and hardened linker flags

scenarios.scenario: Running without a terminal command prints usage and exits cleanly
given: the fswm binary is executed without an additional command-line argument
then: the program prints `usage: printf 'exec fswm <terminal_emulator_command>\n' >> ~/.xinitrc` to stderr
the program exits with status 0
the event loop is not entered

scenarios.scenario: Startup fails when keycodes cannot be resolved
given: the program cannot resolve one or more of Delete, T, or Tab keycodes
then: the program prints `fswm: cannot get keycodes` to stderr
the program exits with failure

scenarios.scenario: Startup fails when the X server connection cannot be established
given: xcb_connection_has_error(connection) > 0
then: the program prints `fswm: cannot connect to the X server` to stderr
the program exits with failure

scenarios.scenario: Startup fails when another window manager owns the root redirect
given: changing the root window event mask returns an X request error
when: the program tries to become the window manager for the root window
then: the program prints `fswm: another window manager is already running` to stderr
the program exits with failure

scenarios.scenario: Successful initialization grabs the supported keybindings
given: startup argument validation passes
the X connection is healthy
no competing window manager is running
then: the root window subscribes to substructure redirect, substructure notify, and property change events
Alt+Tab is grabbed
Alt+Shift+Tab is grabbed
Ctrl+Alt+T is grabbed
Ctrl+Alt+Delete is grabbed

scenarios.scenario: A newly mapped window is tracked and fullscreened to the screen
given: the event loop receives a MapRequest for a window that is not already tracked
then: a client node is appended to the internal doubly linked list
the window is mapped
the window becomes the current focus
the window is configured to x=0, y=0, width=screen width, height=screen height

scenarios.scenario: An already tracked window can be remapped without duplicating the client entry
given: the event loop receives a MapRequest for a window that is already tracked
then: no second client node is created for that window
the existing client is mapped, focused, and resized to fill the screen

scenarios.scenario: Out-of-memory during client creation is reported and the request is ignored
given: the event loop receives a MapRequest for an untracked window
allocating a new client node fails
then: the program prints `fswm: out of memory` to stderr
the window is not appended to the client list

scenarios.scenario: Unmapped or destroyed tracked windows are removed from the client list
given: the event loop receives UnmapNotify or DestroyNotify for a tracked window
then: the matching client is removed from the internal doubly linked list
current and previous focus pointers are cleared if they referred to that client
focus falls back to the previous focus or the head of the list when available

scenarios.scenario: Unmap-style events for unknown windows are ignored
given: the event loop receives UnmapNotify or DestroyNotify for a window that is not tracked
then: the client list is unchanged
no focus update occurs

scenarios.scenario: Alt+Tab cycles focus forward through tracked clients
given: at least one client is tracked
the event loop receives a Tab key press event that reaches the handler
then: focus moves to the next tracked client after the current one
focus wraps to the head of the list when the current client is the tail
the focused window is raised above others
input focus is set to the focused window

scenarios.scenario: Alt+Shift+Tab cycles focus backward through tracked clients
given: at least one client is tracked
when: a KeyPress matching Tab with Alt+Shift is handled
then: focus moves to the previous tracked client before the current one
focus wraps to the tail of the list when needed
the focused window is raised above others
input focus is set to the focused window

scenarios.scenario: Ctrl+Alt+T launches the configured terminal command
given: the program was started with a terminal emulator command in argv[1]
the event loop receives a T key press event that reaches the handler
then: the process forks
the child calls execvp(argv[1], &argv[1])
a failed exec in the child exits with status 1
the parent keeps running the window manager

given: the window manager event loop is running
the event loop receives a Delete key press event that reaches the handler
then: key handling returns a stop signal
the main loop breaks
allocated keycode and XCB resources are freed before the program exits successfully
